pack_name: Law and Legal Sector Compliance Overlay
description: Sector-specific screening controls for Canadian law firms, legal departments, legal-service providers, trust-accounting, client confidentiality, conflict, and client-property documentation.

metadata.scope: Canada sector overlay for Law and Legal. Intended to be applied with canada_core_control_library. Law-society obligations are primarily provincial/territorial and require jurisdiction-specific review.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official Canadian federal/provincial/territorial legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Model Code of Professional Conduct
primary_sources.issuer: Federation of Law Societies of Canada

primary_sources.name: Law Society of Ontario Rules of Professional Conduct
Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA)
Baseline Cyber Security Controls for Small and Medium Organizations

rules.title: Client confidentiality or solicitor-client privilege controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for client confidentiality, solicitor-client privilege, privileged communication, file confidentiality, or permitted-disclosure controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: FLSC Model Code / law society rules

rules.title: Conflict-of-interest screening and consent evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for conflict checks, conflict of interest, informed consent, disclosure, former-client conflict, or screening measures.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, provincial/territorial overlays, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: FLSC Model Code / law society rules

rules.title: Client property, trust-account, or accounting evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for client property, trust account, trust conditions, accounting, safekeeping, or delivery-of-property evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.

rules.title: Client identification or verification evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for client identification, verification, retainer, source of funds, or risk-screening evidence.

rules.title: Legal-file cybersecurity and access-control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for access control, encryption, document management, backups, incident response, or confidentiality safeguards for legal files.
